{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "Hotel Capitol is a modern and contemporary hotel that offers not only luxury but comfort at its best. The three-star hotel is strategically located at the heart of Omole Phase 1, making it easily accessible with excellent road networks to other parts of the mainland and the city of Lagos as a whole. The 32-room Hotel offers tastefully furnished deluxe executive and super executive rooms as well as one- and two-bedroom luxury suites.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Human Resources Officer is responsible for managing all HR functions within the hotel, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and compliance with labor laws.
	The role ensures that the hotel maintains a productive workforce, promotes staff welfare, and supports overall operational efficiency.


Key Duties &amp; Responsibilities
Recruitment &amp; Staffing:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, interviews, selection, onboarding)
	Coordinate with department heads to identify staffing needs
	Prepare employment letters, contracts, and documentation
	Maintain a database of candidates and staff records


Employee Relations:


	Address staff complaints, grievances, and disciplinary matters
	Issue queries, warnings, and disciplinary actions when necessary
	Promote a positive work environment and staff engagement
	Serve as a link between management and employees


Performance Management:


	Monitor employee performance and conduct appraisals
	Support department heads in evaluating staff performance
	Identify training and development needs


Training &amp; Development:


	Organize orientation programs for new employees
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives
	Ensure employees understand hotel policies and procedures
	HR Administration:
	&bull;Maintain accurate employee records and files
	Manage attendance, leave, and shift records
	Prepare HR reports (daily, weekly, monthly)
	Ensure proper documentation and compliance


Payroll &amp; Benefits Support:


	Assist in salary processing and staff benefits administration
	Monitor staff attendance for payroll accuracy
	Handle leave management and records


Compliance &amp; Policy Implementation:


	Ensure compliance with labor laws and hotel policies
	Develop and implement HR policies and procedures
	Support audits and inspections


Health, Safety &amp; Welfare:


	Ensure staff adhere to health and safety regulations
	Handle workplace incidents and report accordingly
	Promote employee welfare programs


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Time taken to fill vacancies
	Staff turnover rate
	Employee satisfaction and retention
	Compliance with HR policies and labor laws
	Timeliness and accuracy of HR documentation
	Effectiveness in handling disciplinary issues


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / BSc in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
	2&ndash;5 years HR experience (hospitality experience is an advantage)
	Knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices
	Membership in HR professional bodies (e.g., CIPM) is an added advantage


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Conflict resolution and problem-solving ability
	Organizational and multitasking skills
	Attention to detail and confidentiality
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office
	Leadership and decision-making skills


Working Conditions:


	Fast-paced hotel environment
	May require extended working hours, including weekends
	Frequent interaction with staff and management


Personal Attributes:


	Professional and approachable
	Fair and firm in decision-making
	High level of integrity and confidentiality
	Proactive and result-driven.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@hotelcapitol.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-07",

Human Resources Officer Job at Hotel Capitol




        
    

        
        Hotel Capitol is a modern and contemporary hotel that offers not only luxury but comfort at its best. The three-star hotel is strategically located at the heart of Omole Phase 1, making it easily accessible with excellent road networks to other parts of the mainland and the city of Lagos as a whole. The 32-room Hotel offers tastefully furnished deluxe executive and super executive rooms as well as one- and two-bedroom luxury suites.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Human Resources Officer is responsible for managing all HR functions within the hotel, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and compliance with labor laws.
	The role ensures that the hotel maintains a productive workforce, promotes staff welfare, and supports overall operational efficiency.


Key Duties & Responsibilities
Recruitment & Staffing:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, interviews, selection, onboarding)
	Coordinate with department heads to identify staffing needs
	Prepare employment letters, contracts, and documentation
	Maintain a database of candidates and staff records


Employee Relations:


	Address staff complaints, grievances, and disciplinary matters
	Issue queries, warnings, and disciplinary actions when necessary
	Promote a positive work environment and staff engagement
	Serve as a link between management and employees


Performance Management:


	Monitor employee performance and conduct appraisals
	Support department heads in evaluating staff performance
	Identify training and development needs


Training & Development:


	Organize orientation programs for new employees
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives
	Ensure employees understand hotel policies and procedures
	HR Administration:
	•Maintain accurate employee records and files
	Manage attendance, leave, and shift records
	Prepare HR reports (daily, weekly, monthly)
	Ensure proper documentation and compliance


Payroll & Benefits Support:


	Assist in salary processing and staff benefits administration
	Monitor staff attendance for payroll accuracy
	Handle leave management and records


Compliance & Policy Implementation:


	Ensure compliance with labor laws and hotel policies
	Develop and implement HR policies and procedures
	Support audits and inspections


Health, Safety & Welfare:


	Ensure staff adhere to health and safety regulations
	Handle workplace incidents and report accordingly
	Promote employee welfare programs


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Time taken to fill vacancies
	Staff turnover rate
	Employee satisfaction and retention
	Compliance with HR policies and labor laws
	Timeliness and accuracy of HR documentation
	Effectiveness in handling disciplinary issues


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / BSc in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
	2–5 years HR experience (hospitality experience is an advantage)
	Knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices
	Membership in HR professional bodies (e.g., CIPM) is an added advantage


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Conflict resolution and problem-solving ability
	Organizational and multitasking skills
	Attention to detail and confidentiality
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office
	Leadership and decision-making skills


Working Conditions:


	Fast-paced hotel environment
	May require extended working hours, including weekends
	Frequent interaction with staff and management


Personal Attributes:


	Professional and approachable
	Fair and firm in decision-making
	High level of integrity and confidentiality
	Proactive and result-driven.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Team Lead - Microfinance Business",
  "description": "Wema Bank Plc is a Nigerian commercial bank that provides a wide range of financial services to individuals, small and medium-sized enterprises, and large corporate organizations, combining traditional banking with strong digital innovation. The bank offers core services such as savings and current accounts, loans, cards, and payment solutions, while also supporting businesses through financing, working capital, and tailored financial advisory. A major part of its strategy is driven by its flagship digital platform, ALAT by Wema, which enables customers to access fully digital banking services, reflecting the bank&rsquo;s focus on convenience, technology, and customer-centric solutions. Overall, Wema Bank positions itself as a forward-thinking institution committed to enhancing financial access, driving digital adoption, and supporting economic growth across various customer segments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Team Lead - Microfinance Business

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time/ Hybrid
Grade: BO - AM

About the Role


	The role is responsible for managing the bank&rsquo;s end?to?end value chain with Microfinance Banks and Institutions by originating and managing relationships that deliver commercial banking solutions, including liabilities, credit lines, and treasury products.
	The candidate will also serve as a trusted advisor on portfolio health, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance, bridging the bank&rsquo;s commercial objectives with the stability of the microfinance ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities


	Acquire and onboard new Microfinance Banks (MFBs) and Institutions while managing and growing existing partner portfolios.
	Drive adoption of banking technology solutions, APIs, and core banking applications for MFI/MFB partners.
	Structure and market tailored wholesale credit, contingent facilities, and liability products aligned to MFB/MFI cash?flow cycles.
	Identify and execute cross?selling opportunities across trade services, treasury, digital payments, and partnerships.
	Develop and manage the go?to?market strategy for the microfinance sector, tracking revenue and balance?sheet performance.
	Lead due diligence for MFB onboarding, assessing credit methodology, portfolio quality, and management strength.
	Monitor partner financial health, asset quality, and capital adequacy to manage and mitigate risk exposure.
	Provide advisory support on credit risk management, product diversification, and pricing strategies.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and guide partners on KYC/AML and consumer protection standards.
	Collaborate on product innovation, track market trends, and represent the bank in industry and regulatory forums.


Job Requirements


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field. An MBA or master&#39;s degree is strongly preferred.
	Experience: 8&ndash;12 years of experience in commercial banking, financial institutions group (FIG), or microfinance lending.

Team Lead - Microfinance Business Job at Wema Bank Plc




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Wema Bank Plc is a Nigerian commercial bank that provides a wide range of financial services to individuals, small and medium-sized enterprises, and large corporate organizations, combining traditional banking with strong digital innovation. The bank offers core services such as savings and current accounts, loans, cards, and payment solutions, while also supporting businesses through financing, working capital, and tailored financial advisory. A major part of its strategy is driven by its flagship digital platform, ALAT by Wema, which enables customers to access fully digital banking services, reflecting the bank’s focus on convenience, technology, and customer-centric solutions. Overall, Wema Bank positions itself as a forward-thinking institution committed to enhancing financial access, driving digital adoption, and supporting economic growth across various customer segments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Team Lead - Microfinance Business

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time/ Hybrid
Grade: BO - AM

About the Role


	The role is responsible for managing the bank’s end?to?end value chain with Microfinance Banks and Institutions by originating and managing relationships that deliver commercial banking solutions, including liabilities, credit lines, and treasury products.
	The candidate will also serve as a trusted advisor on portfolio health, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance, bridging the bank’s commercial objectives with the stability of the microfinance ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities


	Acquire and onboard new Microfinance Banks (MFBs) and Institutions while managing and growing existing partner portfolios.
	Drive adoption of banking technology solutions, APIs, and core banking applications for MFI/MFB partners.
	Structure and market tailored wholesale credit, contingent facilities, and liability products aligned to MFB/MFI cash?flow cycles.
	Identify and execute cross?selling opportunities across trade services, treasury, digital payments, and partnerships.
	Develop and manage the go?to?market strategy for the microfinance sector, tracking revenue and balance?sheet performance.
	Lead due diligence for MFB onboarding, assessing credit methodology, portfolio quality, and management strength.
	Monitor partner financial health, asset quality, and capital adequacy to manage and mitigate risk exposure.
	Provide advisory support on credit risk management, product diversification, and pricing strategies.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and guide partners on KYC/AML and consumer protection standards.
	Collaborate on product innovation, track market trends, and represent the bank in industry and regulatory forums.


Job Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s degree in finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field. An MBA or master's degree is strongly preferred.
	Experience: 8–12 years of experience in commercial banking, financial institutions group (FIG), or microfinance    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "TalenMO is a professional recruitment and talent solutions firm dedicated to sourcing, screening, and placing the right people in the right roles across various MSMEs industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Magodo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	As a Business Development Executive you will play a key role in driving the growth and success of our portfolio ventures impact projects and hub services
	This multifaceted role combines venture-building support proposal writing client interfacing and the sales of our services to startups tech-enabled MSMEs and other stakeholders in our expanding community and beyond


Job Responsibilities


	Provide strategic and operational support to our portfolio ventures assisting in scaling their operations and achieving business milestones
	Work closely with startups and tech MSMEs in our ecosystem offering business advice mentorship and access to relevant resources
	Conduct in-depth market research to identify new business opportunities emerging trends and competitive insights relevant to our ventures and hub services
	Analyze market data to develop growth strategies and position our services to meet the demands of the market
	Write compelling proposals concept notes and grant applications that clearly articulate our value proposition and project impact
	Develop presentations and pitches to communicate the unique benefits of our services to potential clients and partners
	Act as the primary point of contact for clients ensuring clear communication and strong relationship management throughout the project lifecycle
	Identify client needs and work collaboratively with internal teams to develop tailored solutions that address their specific challenges
	Develop and implement sales strategies aimed at increasing revenue from our hub services including training programs consulting and workspace solutions
	Meet or exceed sales targets by effectively selling our services to startups tech MSMEs and other businesses
	Identify and cultivate strategic partnerships that can enhance our service offerings or expand our market reach
	Lead negotiations and finalize partnership agreements that align with 8thGear&rsquo;s business objectives
	Maintain accurate records of business development activities client interactions and deal progress in CRM systems
	Provide regular reports to management on sales performance market trends and business development initiatives


General Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Communications Marketing PR or related fields
	3&ndash;6 years in Business Development Sales Communications or Growth roles
	Proven record closing business not just awareness
	Proven track record of running high-performing campaigns with verifiable results
	Strong understanding of marketing funnels conversion optimization and CRM tools preferably Zoho
	Skilled in Ads Management Meta Business Suite Google Ads LinkedIn Campaign Manager
	Proficiency in social media management and analytics tools
	Excellent content planning copywriting and storytelling abilities
	Ability to lead a small creative team and manage multiple content pipelines
	Strong analytical skills with ability to optimize campaigns based on data
	Experience in coordinating community events or online engagement initiatives
	High curiosity creativity and commitment to staying updated on content trends
	Entrepreneurial mindset and willingness to take initiative.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV to:&nbsp;temiloluwa.talenmo@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development Executive Job at TalenMO




        
    

        
        TalenMO is a professional recruitment and talent solutions firm dedicated to sourcing, screening, and placing the right people in the right roles across various MSMEs industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location: Magodo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As a Business Development Executive you will play a key role in driving the growth and success of our portfolio ventures impact projects and hub services
	This multifaceted role combines venture-building support proposal writing client interfacing and the sales of our services to startups tech-enabled MSMEs and other stakeholders in our expanding community and beyond


Job Responsibilities


	Provide strategic and operational support to our portfolio ventures assisting in scaling their operations and achieving business milestones
	Work closely with startups and tech MSMEs in our ecosystem offering business advice mentorship and access to relevant resources
	Conduct in-depth market research to identify new business opportunities emerging trends and competitive insights relevant to our ventures and hub services
	Analyze market data to develop growth strategies and position our services to meet the demands of the market
	Write compelling proposals concept notes and grant applications that clearly articulate our value proposition and project impact
	Develop presentations and pitches to communicate the unique benefits of our services to potential clients and partners
	Act as the primary point of contact for clients ensuring clear communication and strong relationship management throughout the project lifecycle
	Identify client needs and work collaboratively with internal teams to develop tailored solutions that address their specific challenges
	Develop and implement sales strategies aimed at increasing revenue from our hub services including training programs consulting and workspace solutions
	Meet or exceed sales targets by effectively selling our services to startups tech MSMEs and other businesses
	Identify and cultivate strategic partnerships that can enhance our service offerings or expand our market reach
	Lead negotiations and finalize partnership agreements that align with 8thGear’s business objectives
	Maintain accurate records of business development activities client interactions and deal progress in CRM systems
	Provide regular reports to management on sales performance market trends and business development initiatives


General Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Communications Marketing PR or related fields
	3–6 years in Business Development Sales Communications or Growth roles
	Proven record closing business not just awareness
	Proven track record of running high-performing campaigns with verifiable results
	Strong understanding of marketing funnels conversion optimization and CRM tools preferably Zoho
	Skilled in Ads Management Meta Business Suite Google Ads LinkedIn Campaign Manager
	Proficiency in social media management and analytics tools
	Excellent content planning copywriting and storytelling abilities
	Ability to lead a small creative team and manage multiple content pipelines
	Strong analytical skills with ability to optimize campaigns based on data
	Experience in coordinating community events or online engagement initiatives
	High curiosity creativity and commitment to staying updated on content trends
	Entrepreneurial mindset and willingness to take initiative.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Leen Premium Limited
content:
Leen Premium Limited is a dynamic and quality-driven company committed to delivering excellence in the production and distribution of premium bottled water and related products. Established with a focus on health, safety, and customer satisfaction, the company operates in compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Storekeeper

Job Location: Mushin, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Store Keeper is responsible for managing the receipt, storage, and issuance of materials and products.
	The role ensures proper inventory control, accurate record-keeping, and prevention of stock losses or damages.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming materials and goods.
	Properly store items to ensure safety and easy identification.
	Issue materials and products based on approved requisitions.
	Maintain accurate stock records (manual or computerized).
	Conduct regular stock counts and reconciliation.
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages or excesses.
	Ensure proper labeling and arrangement of items in the store.
	Prevent loss, theft, or damage of stock.
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the store.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND in Accounting, Business Administration, or related field.
	Basic knowledge of inventory management.
	Good numerical and record-keeping skills.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.
	Ability to use Microsoft Excel or inventory software.
	Good integrity and accountability.


Experience:


	2–4 years of experience in a storekeeping or warehouse role.
	Experience in a manufacturing or FMCG environment is an added advantage.


Remuneration
N70,000 – N100,000 monthly (depending on experience).

Application Closing Date: 20th May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Security personnel

Job Location: Mushin, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Security Officer is responsible for protecting the company’s premises, staff, and assets by maintaining a secure environment.
	The role involves monitoring activities, controlling access, and responding to security incidents.


Key Responsibilities


	Guard and patrol company premises to prevent theft, vandalism, or unauthorized access
	Monitor surveillance cameras and security systems
	Control entry and exit of staff, visitors, and vehicles
	Conduct regular security checks and inspections
	Respond promptly to alarms, emergencies, and incidents
	Maintain accurate incident and daily activity reports
	Enforce company safety and security policies


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE / OND qualification
	Good physical fitness and alertness
	Strong observation and attention to detail
	Ability to read and write reports clearly
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic knowledge of security procedures


Experience:


	1–3 years of experience in a security role
	Experience in industrial, manufacturing, or corporate environments is an added advantage
	Prior training in security operations or certifications is a plus.


Salary
?60,000 – ?75,000 monthly (depending on experience).

Application Closing Date: 15th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

3.) Company Driver

Job Location: Mushin, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Company Driver is responsible for the safe and efficient transportation of staff, goods, and company materials
	The role requires maintaining assigned vehicles in good condition and ensuring compliance with road safety regulations.


Key Responsibilities


	Drive company vehicles to transport staff, goods, and materials as required
	Ensure timely pickup and delivery schedules
	Maintain cleanliness and proper condition of the assigned vehicle
	Carry out routine vehicle checks (oil, water, brakes, tyres, etc.)
	Report any faults or mechanical issues promptly
	Keep accurate records of trips, fuel usage, and maintenance
	Adhere strictly to traffic laws and company driving policies
	Assist with loading and unloading when necessary.


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE qualification
	Valid driver’s license
	Good knowledge of Lagos roads and traffic regulations
	Ability to read and write basic reports
	Good attitude, punctuality, and reliability
	Strong sense of responsibility and safety awareness.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–5 years of driving experience
	Experience as a company or commercial driver is an added advantage
	Clean driving record with no major traffic violations.


Working Conditions:


	May require early resumption, late closing, or weekend duties
	Occasional long-distance trips.


Remuneration


	Salary: N70,000 – N100,000 monthly (depending on experience)
	Opportunity for long-term employment.


Application Closing Date: 5th May, 2026.

Method of Appliction
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "The Brightview Schools is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Admin Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Apete Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are searching for a trustworthy and personable School Adimin Officer to join our learning institution.&nbsp;The school Admin&#39;s responsibilities include handling all allocated tasks, answering questions and queries, understanding the school&rsquo;s policies, assisting with the recruitment of new staff members, and communicating with parents, teachers, and students as required. You should be able to take charge of your department and provide the necessary supervision.
	To be successful as a school Admin officer, you should be detail-oriented without losing sight of the bigger picture. Outstanding candidates have the capacity to handle multiple responsibilities and don&#39;t lose their nerve under pressure.


Responsibilities


	Understanding and reviewing the way the school and all its departments function.
	Conducting research on professional and ongoing learning workshops and sharing these opportunities with teachers and other staff.
	Attending meetings and scheduling appropriate staff team building sessions.
	Ensuring fair allocation of budget and resources.
	Managing school security, caretakers, and other ground staff.
	Assisting with the hiring, recruitment, training, and onboarding of new teachers.
	Establishing and maintaining good relationships with parents, students, and staff at other schools.
	Preparing and submitting drafts of proposals, financial reports, and other documents.


Requirements


	Must be computer literate
	Must be a resident of Apete Ibadan.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;thebrightviewschools@gmail.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the email.",

Admin Officer Job at the Brickhall School




        
    

        
        The Brightview Schools is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Admin Officer

Job Location: Apete Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are searching for a trustworthy and personable School Adimin Officer to join our learning institution. The school Admin's responsibilities include handling all allocated tasks, answering questions and queries, understanding the school’s policies, assisting with the recruitment of new staff members, and communicating with parents, teachers, and students as required. You should be able to take charge of your department and provide the necessary supervision.
	To be successful as a school Admin officer, you should be detail-oriented without losing sight of the bigger picture. Outstanding candidates have the capacity to handle multiple responsibilities and don't lose their nerve under pressure.


Responsibilities


	Understanding and reviewing the way the school and all its departments function.
	Conducting research on professional and ongoing learning workshops and sharing these opportunities with teachers and other staff.
	Attending meetings and scheduling appropriate staff team building sessions.
	Ensuring fair allocation of budget and resources.
	Managing school security, caretakers, and other ground staff.
	Assisting with the hiring, recruitment, training, and onboarding of new teachers.
	Establishing and maintaining good relationships with parents, students, and staff at other schools.
	Preparing and submitting drafts of proposals, financial reports, and other documents.


Requirements


	Must be computer literate
	Must be a resident of Apete Ibadan.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.
